old_claim.note: Public Museum Catalogs provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the sourced industrial-design scope is mass-produced product and corporate design, not museum collection cataloging.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica defines industrial design as design of mass-produced consumer products and traces modern origins through Behrens, AEG products, packaging, advertising, and corporate identity.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors industrial design in mass-produced product, branding, packaging, and corporate-client design rather than public museum cataloging.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This removes a museum-catalog precedent that could launder collection documentation into a product-design profession whose source-backed lineage is industrial manufacture and corporate design.

ontology_before: Industrial Design Profession depended on Printed Cabinet Catalogs, mixing museum collection documentation into product-design professionalization.
ontology_after: Industrial Design Profession keeps a product-design and mass-production scope without a public museum catalog dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: industrial_design_profession no longer directly depends on public_museum_catalogs.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to museum exhibition design or collection-catalog production.
A source showed public museum cataloging was a direct prerequisite for the industrial design profession.
The public_museum_catalogs node were broadened away from printed cabinet and collection catalogs.
short_reason: Museum collection catalogs are outside the product-design profession scope.
